auditor: Ernst & Young (member firm of Ernst & Young Global Limited)
opinion: Unqualified (clean) — presents fairly in all material respects

revenue_recognition.standard: ASC 606 Revenue from Contracts with Customers
revenue_recognition.royalties: Recognized based on monthly franchisees' retail sales; equals greater of stated percentage of gross sales and stated minimum
revenue_recognition.vendor_allowances: Recognized based on actual periodic purchases and contractual terms, when consideration is fixed/determinable and collection reasonably assured
revenue_recognition.franchisee_contributions: Includes initial fees, promotional item sales, marketing/admin contributions, other. Recognized when earned and services rendered. Initial fees deferred and recognized over license term.

use_of_estimates.description: Estimates include allowance for expected credit losses. Actual results could differ.

cash.description: Bank balances including bank overdrafts with balances that fluctuate from positive to overdrawn

financial_instruments.fair_value_hierarchy: All instruments Level 1 as of Dec 31, 2024 and 2023
instruments_included: Amounts to be remitted to franchisees
Advances to/from parent and common control

property_and_equipment.notes: Depreciation method and useful lives not explicitly stated in notes. Amounts are minimal.

lease_accounting.short_term_lease_election: Leases ≤12 months: no ROU/liability recognized, expense on straight-line basis
lease_accounting.renewal_options: Not included until reasonably certain to be exercised

advertising_fund_accounting.treatment: Contributions received must be spent on advertising/marketing. No net income recognized. Excess contributions recorded as deferred revenue.
advertising_fund_accounting.expense_timing: Expensed when advertisements first aired or displayed; all other promotional costs expensed as incurred

income_taxes.valuation_allowance: Full valuation allowance recorded against all deferred tax assets for all years
income_taxes.filing_status: Member of consolidated group with parent; allocated taxes as if separate taxpayer
income_taxes.tax_effect_pass_through: Any tax effect passed to parent for equal consideration if realizable on consolidated basis

comprehensive_income.treatment: Comprehensive loss equals net loss for all periods (no other comprehensive income items)

impairment.notes: No specific impairment policy disclosed in notes. No impairment charges recorded.

note1_organization.description: LLC formed April 22, 2020 in Delaware. Wholly-owned by Mondofix USA LLC, itself wholly-owned by Mondofix Inc. (Canada). Franchisor of car body repair shops under ProColor brand.
note1_organization.going_concern_2023: Company incurred annual losses since creation. Ability to continue as going concern depends on: (1) maintaining suitable financing through parent support, (2) growing franchisee network for positive cash flows, (3) honoring payables. Parent committed to financial support through advances/contributions for at least 12 months from subsequent events note date.
note1_organization.going_concern_2024: Going concern language REMOVED from 2024 notes. No longer present.

note3_cash_in_trust.description: Held in separate non-interest bearing bank account. Amounts from insurers to be remitted to franchisees.

note5_accounts_payable.notes: Accrued salaries dropped significantly ($201K→$83K) while trade payables increased.

intercompany_terms.advances_to_parent: Non-interest bearing, not repayable before January 1, 2026
intercompany_terms.advances_to_from_common_control: Non-interest bearing, not repayable before January 1, 2026

credit_risk.management: Pre-approved franchisees, regular monitoring of collections

note10_subsequent_events.material_events: Financial statements reflect material items arising after balance sheet date but before issuance date

variable_interest_entities.notes: No VIE disclosures in financial statements

recent_accounting_pronouncements.2024: No accounting pronouncements adopted with significant effect
recent_accounting_pronouncements.2023: ASU 2016-13 Financial Instruments—Credit Losses (Topic 326) adopted; no material impact
